_comment: Canonical EIA/MIL ceramic dielectric code -> CAS ceramic technology class. Single source of truth for downstream chemistry-family logic (e.g. cross-reference ranking) so consumers stop hardcoding the code list. The chemistry-family enum itself is CAS/schemas/capacitor.json#/properties/technology; the per-part code goes in its dielectricCode field. Class boundaries follow the EIA RS-198 second/third characters: low-temp X/Y/Z, high-temp 4..8, capacitance-tolerance R(±15%)/S(±22%)/T(+22/-33%)/U(+22/-56%)/V(+22/-82%). Class-1 = temperature-stable (C0G/NP0/U2J); class-2 = high-K stable-ish (R/S/T tolerances); class-3 = high-K unstable (U/V tolerances, e.g. Z5U/Y5V variants).
